Casual consumer foot traffic was lower, but scheduled meetings jumped +71% and media coverage still grew +6% — confirming a successful pivot toward high-value professional relationships.

02 Traffic Analysis

Lower volume, much higher quality

Overall traffic dipped, but booked appointments jumped 170 → 292 (+71%) — the floor skewed decisively toward high-value professional visitors.

03 Visitor distribution by region

Taiwan and APAC drove the floor

RegionAppointmentsShareBooth visitorsShare
APAC19968%14030%
TW4114%60256%
EU2810%396%
SAC124%223%
JP72%121%
KR41%182%
Others00%162%
Total291100%833100%

Local Taiwan visitors were the majority of the booth floor (56%), followed by APAC (30%) and EU (6%). Despite proactive SAC outreach, US client & media participation stayed limited (~30).

On US media presence

US trade media do not prioritize COMPUTEX — only consumer-facing outlets such as Tom's Hardware and PCMag cover 2C products. Other media titles will not fly in. Recommended approach: use live-streamed press events to reach US local media directly, rather than expecting on-the-floor attendance.

Source: COMPUTEX 2026 Daily Report
04 Booth Design

Smaller booth, bigger presence

To overcome the smaller footprint, we used a tall vertical LED structure that drew strong praise from clients and media. Overall finish and presentation quality were also significantly elevated.

04 Booth & experience — highlights
05 Visitor interests by solution

What the floor asked about

Storage

  • GS object storage — scale-out S3 to replace costly public cloud; clients awaiting launch
  • PAS7700 active-active Hot Tier for VM/AI; wants Sync Replication + Metro Cluster
  • FS all-flash — first pick for HPC / research (NICT); needs 50/100GbE
  • Ask: S3 Immutable / Object Lock (30-yr WORM), auto hot-cold tiering
Deployments: BenQ Materials · Macronix · Malaysia AI · Japan NICT

Data Protection

  • ActiveProtect appliance — “license-free HW+SW” model drew heavy interest
  • VMware exodus — migrating to PVE / Nutanix / Arcfra; needs agentless backup
  • Ask: native Oracle 19c & MS SQL hot backup + transaction-log recovery
  • DP5200 / DP7400 praised for SMB & dual-site DR
Deployments: 104 Corp · Macronix · EVA Air (M365)

Video Surveillance

  • DVA AI demo wowed — Re-ID tracking & semantic search; asks DVA3000
  • Access control × Surveillance integration a top theme (PoE, fail-secure, fire/attendance)
  • Ask: 3 cm hand-action precision; full JSON API for traffic command systems
  • Most concrete demand of the show
Deployments: CECI (190+ cam ALPR) · Gov 200–300ch CMS · Arbor POC

AI & Productivity

  • Synology Office — MS-compat pain eased; Q4 direct MS-file edit lifted adoption
  • Local DSM Agent / semantic search praised (no cloud) — but high GPU bar
  • Ask: Notion-like collab; AD/ACL permission inheritance; Chat / Calendar bots
  • Sensitive sectors ban cloud uploads — on-prem compliance is the draw
Deployments: Fintech 650→on-prem · TSMC supplier · Gov legal 1000-ppl AI KB

Digital Life (DLG)

  • BeeDrive instant backup liked — strong ask for phone-direct backup (no PC)
  • BeeCamera detection a hotspot — pet tracking (incl. exotic) + 3rd-party IP cams
  • Ask: mobile / offline Deep Search; index video keyframes & Outlook .pst
  • BeeStation eyed by SMBs to replace cloud subscriptions
Source: COMPUTEX 2026 Solution Feedback Report (CHT)
06 Press Event

119 attendees — steady, and AI-focused

  • 119 attendance, holding against last year's 120. Venue was not ideal — — still seeking a more presentable space.
  • Next venue spec: classroom seating for 150, a video wall over 5 m wide, professional lighting.
  • Media interest centered on AI — our positioning, security & governance. Keep sessions under 30 min.
  • APM 2.0 drew notably lower interest — not a brand-new product line.

Media Coordination Highlights by Region
EU (145) — Booth-visit-heavy (55%): strong floor presence from FR, CEE, Benelux media at the show.
KR (77) — Highest press event + interview ratio (35% + 8%): best engagement quality of all regions.
JP (83) — Dominated by PR pickup (87%): wide syndication from a compact media delegation.
APAC (356) — Balanced mix: PR-led (59%) but also the highest absolute booth visits (65) and press coverage (49).
Source: Coverage Breakdown  |  2026 COMPUTEX Media Coordination
07 Exposure — what they wrote about

The AI message landed — in their words

  • Media focus centered on AI — DSM Agent and APM 2.0 AI-powered proactive defense.
  • On-premises setup, data control & digital sovereignty were widely picked up — the market received our AI data-management positioning.

“Local and sovereign AI is reshaping the storage and office automation market.”

ZDNet.fr — B2B

“DSM is becoming a platform for on-premises AI and cyber resilience.”

TechBusiness — B2B (IT)

“Data sovereignty, AI reshape enterprise storage demand.”

The Manila Times — Business (PH)

“Synology used COMPUTEX 2026 to push ActiveProtect past its own appliances.”

StorageReview — Business (US)
08 Forum Keynote

Synology’s first time on the COMPUTEX keynote stage

  • Goal: expand brand awareness well beyond our existing customer base.
  • Targeted 600–800 audience; actual turnout reached ~300–400.
  • Setup was excellent, but COMPUTEX's recruitment channels under-delivered.
  • Recommendation: re-evaluate participation in this event next year.
Q
Should we keep the keynote for 2027?
Reconsider — the COMPUTEX recruitment channel under-delivered (300–400 vs. 600–800 target). Only renew if audience-sourcing improves or we bring our own livestream audience. The production quality is there; the distribution is not.
09 HQ Media Tour

HQ tour just scratched the surface

  • The European branch strongly requested an HQ tour — ~40 media visited the office on June 1.
  • We hosted behind-the-scenes sessions on SIRT & GAIA, plus a TPKA (incl. Google) surveillance case study.
  • Strong interaction around DSM Agent, vibe-coding, AI-gineer; specific Qs on TPKA's model & topology.
Q
Should we keep the HQ media tour?
Yes, but restructure it — the tour format is too passive. Replace the group walkthrough with themed 1-on-1 or small-group interviews with HQ product owners. EU media engagement was strong; the format just needs to match the depth they want.

▼ −23% YoY
  • Lower cost driven by the smaller booth footprint and an external press-event venue.
  • Our buildup partner delivered exceptional value — recommend continuing next year.
  • To elevate the press event we'll add budget for an LED wall + pro lighting and 150-person classroom layout.
  • Adjust press-conference budget to ≈ NT$1–1.5 M.
11 The verdict

Should we do it again in 2027?

Attend COMPUTEX?
YES — strategic anchor

COMPUTEX remains a crucial gathering point for channels and media. A leaner footprint still delivered +71% meetings and +6% coverage. Continue — meeting-led, not floor-traffic-led.

Press Event?
YES — escalate the game

119 attendees held steady, but the venue and production must match the brand. Upgrade the venue vibe, add a 5 m+ LED wall with pro lighting, and introduce live streaming to extend reach well beyond the room.

HQ Tour?
YES — but redesign it

Retire the group walkthrough. Replace with themed behind-the-scenes interviews — small groups or 1-on-1s with HQ product owners on specific topics. EU media showed strong appetite; the format just needs to match the depth they’re looking for.
